suggestions.description: Consider adding error codes for different failure types
suggestions.details: Could differentiate between wrong password, account locked, and other errors

suggestions.description: Consider logging lockout events for security audit trail
suggestions.details: Log when account is locked due to failed attempts for security monitoring

strengths: Perfect architecture alignment - IPC commands in proper layer (src-tauri/src/commands/auth.rs)
100% acceptance criteria coverage - all 8 criteria mapped to specific tasks
Correct command naming matches frontend calls (derive_master_key, verify_password, is_master_key_set)
Password hash persistence implemented (Phase 1: load_from_file, set_password with persistence, PasswordManager::load)
Proper state management with PasswordManager::load() instead of ::new()
Comprehensive testing plan covering all user flows (首次启动, 设置密码, 重启, 验证, 锁定)
Detailed implementation examples reduce implementation risk
Security considerations addressed (Argon2id, random salt, file permissions, zeroize)

fixes_applied.before: Password hash only in memory, lost on restart
fixes_applied.after: Phase 1 adds load_from_file(), set_password() persistence, PasswordManager::load() (lines 54-65, 208-261)
Changed to PasswordManager::load() which takes no parameters (line 131, 297-299)

overall_assessment: The plan has been significantly improved after addressing all critical issues. Command naming now matches frontend expectations, password persistence is properly implemented, state management is correct, and return types are serializable. The plan demonstrates excellent understanding of Tauri IPC patterns, clean architecture, and security best practices. With a score of 95/100, this plan is approved for implementation.
recommendation: APPROVED - Proceed to Phase 2 (execute-plan). All blocking issues resolved. Suggestions are optional enhancements that can be addressed in future iterations.
